title: pseudognaphalium andicola
intro: Pseudognaphalium andicola, also known as Andean cudweed or Chilean rabbit tobacco, is a plant species in the daisy family native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting wound healing. Pseudognaphalium andicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
